category_title: Students with mild intellectual disabilities exhibiting academic engagement, off-task, or disruptive problem behaviors
minimal_context: The student is copying sentences from the board into their notebook during a classroom activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please copy the sentences from the board into your notebook.

STUDENT: (Looks at the board, but then starts playing with pencils instead of writing.)

TUTOR: Keep trying to write.

STUDENT: (Stops looking at the board, starts looking around the classroom, and attends to other things irrelevant to the lesson.)

TUTOR: Focus on your notebook.

STUDENT: (Turns completely away from the notebook and looks outside through the window, continuing to play with the pencil without copying anything.)

options.A: Remain available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near the student and then speaking quietly and gently touching their shoulder.
options.B: Act persistently without becoming angry or insulting the student in public, ensuring they receive neither an irritated response nor an accusation, but rather patient restraint.
options.C: Make adjustments in the instructional program, such as preparing appropriate tasks or assignments for the student to complete during the activity.
options.D: Establish a classroom norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for the evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during the activity.
